findings.evidence_dynamic: Header 'X-Frame-Options' is missing. Risk: Clickjacking attacks — page can be embedded in iframes
findings.remediation: To remediate the X-Frame-Options header vulnerability, add the 'X-Frame-Options' header to the HTTP response with a value of 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' to prevent clickjacking attacks. For example, in Apache, this can be achieved by adding the following line to the .htaccess file: `Header set X-Frame-Options "DENY"`. Alternatively, in a Python Flask application, you can use the `response.headers` dictionary to set the header, such as `response.headers['X-Frame-Options'] = 'DENY'`.

findings.evidence_dynamic: Header 'Content-Security-Policy' is missing. Risk: No XSS mitigation policy — scripts from any source allowed
findings.remediation: To remediate the Content-Security-Policy header vulnerability, add the 'Content-Security-Policy' header to the server's response with a suitable policy, such as 'default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://cdn.example.com;'. For example, in Apache, this can be achieved by adding the line 'Header set Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://cdn.example.com;"' to the .htaccess file or httpd.conf. This will help mitigate XSS attacks by only allowing scripts to be loaded from trusted sources.

findings.evidence_dynamic: Header 'Strict-Transport-Security' is missing. Risk: HTTP downgrade attacks — HTTPS not enforced
findings.remediation: To remediate the Security Misconfiguration vulnerability due to the missing 'Strict-Transport-Security' header, the web server should be configured to include this header in its responses. This can be achieved by adding the following directive to the server's configuration file, for example, in Apache: `Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"`. This sets the Strict-Transport-Security header with a maximum age of one year, enforcing HTTPS for the website and its subdomains.

findings.evidence_dynamic: Header 'X-Content-Type-Options' is missing. Risk: MIME-type sniffing — browser may misinterpret content
findings.remediation: To remediate the missing X-Content-Type-Options header, add the following line to your web server configuration or application code: `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`. In Apache, this can be achieved by adding the line `Header set X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"` to your .htaccess file or httpd.conf. This will instruct browsers to respect the Content-Type header and prevent MIME-type sniffing, thereby reducing the risk of content misinterpretation.

findings.evidence_dynamic: Header 'Referrer-Policy' is missing. Risk: URL data leaked to third parties via Referer header
findings.remediation: To remediate the Referrer-Policy header vulnerability, add the 'Referrer-Policy' header to the server's response with a suitable directive, such as 'same-origin' or 'no-referrer'. This can be achieved by adding the following line to the server's configuration file or HTTP headers: `Referrer-Policy: same-origin`. For example, in Apache, this can be done by adding the line `Header set Referrer-Policy "same-origin"` to the .htaccess file or Apache configuration.

findings.evidence_dynamic: Header 'Permissions-Policy' is missing. Risk: No browser feature restrictions defined
findings.remediation: To remediate the 'Permissions-Policy' header vulnerability, add the 'Permissions-Policy' header to the server's response, defining the features that can be used within a web page. For example, in Apache, this can be achieved by adding the following line to the `.htaccess` file: `Header set Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(self), microphone=(self)"`. This sets the 'Permissions-Policy' header to restrict geolocation and microphone access to the current origin (self).
